rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical bromide-mediated aminooxygenation of N-vinylbenzamides with pyrazoles to give 2-aryl-4,5-dihydrooxazoles bearing a pyrazolyl group
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Br- to Br2 (bromide acts as redox mediator); Br2 then reacts with the enamide N-H to generate an N-Br intermediate which homolyzes/ionizes to N-centered radical and ultimately a carbocation; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: vinyl-stabilized N-centered radical (intermediate C, from homolysis of N-Br)

electron_flow: Br- is anodically oxidized (2e-) to Br2, which brominates the enamide N-H to give N-Br intermediate A; loss of Br- and a further 1e- anodic oxidation generates an N-acyliminium cation D that cyclizes to oxazolinium E/F; pyrazole adds to the cation and deprotonation furnishes product 3, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 yield dropped to 37%; radical adduct 46 and hydrolysates 47 and 48 detected by HRMS, consistent with N-centered/vinyl radical intermediate C

probe_reagents.name: pyrazole (carbocation trap)
probe_reagents.observation: Carbocation-trapped product 49 plus hydrolysates 47 and 50 detected by HRMS in absence of nBu4NBF4, supporting cation D

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of nBu4NBr / 1 / 2
probe_reagents.observation: Br- oxidizes at 0.36 and 0.80 V; substrate 1 and pyrazole 2 show no oxidation peaks 0-0.80 V; catalytic current observed when 1 is added to nBu4NBr, supporting Br- as the mediator

notes: Authors invoke both radical (C/B) and cationic (D/E/F) intermediates connected by further anodic oxidation (radical-polar crossover). HRMS-detected adducts are the principal evidence; no DFT, KIE, or EPR reported. Trace benzamide observed during scale-up also pointed toward involvement of C/D.
